entries.name: Warner Bros. Movie World
entries.desc: A film-themed park on Australia's Gold Coast featuring rides and shows based on Warner Bros. and DC characters. It is one of the region's cluster of major theme parks.

photo.alt: Grand entrance plaza of Warner Bros. Movie World on the Gold Coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: warner-bros-movie-world-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Grand Entrance - Warner Bros. Movie World.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Grand_Entrance_-_Warner_Bros._Movie_World.jpg -->

entries.name: Canada's Wonderland
entries.desc: Canada's largest amusement park, just north of Toronto, with one of the biggest roller-coaster collections in the country and an attached water park. A man-made mountain marks its central landmark.

photo.alt: Wonder Mountain, the man-made mountain landmark at Canada's Wonderland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: wonder-mountain-canadas-wonderland.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Wonder Mountain.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Wonder_Mountain.jpg -->

entries.name: Shanghai Disneyland
entries.desc: Mainland China's first Disney resort park, opened in 2016 and centered on the Enchanted Storybook Castle, the largest of any Disney park. It mixes classic Disney attractions with China-specific design.

photo.alt: Enchanted Storybook Castle at Shanghai Disneyland
photo.credit: <!-- photo: shanghai-disneyland-enchanted-storybook-castle.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Enchanted Storybook Castle of Shanghai Disneyland.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Enchanted_Storybook_Castle_of_Shanghai_Disneyland.jpg -->

entries.name: Universal Beijing Resort
entries.desc: Universal's first park in China and its largest globally by area, opened in 2021 with themed lands spanning Harry Potter, Jurassic World, Transformers and Kung Fu Panda. It anchors a growing resort district.

photo.alt: Main entrance of Universal Studios Beijing
photo.credit: <!-- photo: universal-beijing-resort-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Entrance of Universal Studios Beijing (20210907121707).jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entrance_of_Universal_Studios_Beijing_(20210907121707).jpg -->

entries.name: Chimelong Ocean Kingdom
entries.desc: A marine-life theme park on Hengqin Island near Macau, combining large aquarium habitats with roller coasters and shows. It has ranked among the most visited theme parks in the world.

photo.alt: Whale shark aquarium habitat at Chimelong Ocean Kingdom
photo.credit: <!-- photo: chimelong-ocean-kingdom-whale-shark-aquarium.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "ChimelongOceanKingdom WhaleSharkAquarium1.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:ChimelongOceanKingdom_WhaleSharkAquarium1.jpg -->

entries.name: Tivoli Gardens
entries.desc: A historic pleasure garden in central Copenhagen, opened in 1843 and among the oldest operating amusement parks in the world. It pairs vintage rides with landscaped gardens, concerts and seasonal illuminations.

photo.alt: Main entrance of Tivoli Gardens in Copenhagen
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tivoli-gardens-main-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Tivoli Gardens - main entrance.jpg" by Roger Wollstadt · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tivoli_Gardens_-_main_entrance.jpg -->

entries.name: Disneyland Paris
entries.desc: Europe's most visited theme park resort, east of Paris, centered on Sleeping Beauty Castle. It is one of the continent's leading tourist attractions and is easily reached from central Paris by rail.

photo.alt: Sleeping Beauty Castle at Disneyland Paris
photo.credit: <!-- photo: disneyland-paris-sleeping-beauty-castle.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Disneyland Paryż 2013 (13).JPG" by Kapsuglan · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Disneyland_Pary%C5%BC_2013_(13).JPG -->

entries.name: Parc Astérix
entries.desc: A French park north of Paris themed on the Asterix comic books and ancient Gaul, mixing coaster-heavy thrills with humor drawn from the series. It is one of France's most popular home-grown parks.

photo.alt: Goudurix roller coaster at Parc Astérix
photo.credit: <!-- photo: parc-asterix-goudurix-coaster.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Goudurix.JPG" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Goudurix.JPG -->

entries.name: Puy du Fou
entries.desc: A history-themed park in western France built around large-scale live performances rather than rides, staging shows drawn from Roman, medieval and other eras. Its productions have won international theme-park awards.

photo.alt: The themed castle grounds at Puy du Fou, Les Epesses, France
photo.credit: <!-- photo: puy-du-fou-castle.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Puy-du-Fou-3.JPG" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Puy-du-Fou-3.JPG -->

entries.name: Europa-Park
entries.desc: Germany's largest theme park, organized into areas themed after European countries. It is one of the most visited parks in Europe and is frequently rated among the best in the world by industry awards.

photo.alt: Main entrance of Europa-Park in Rust
photo.credit: <!-- photo: europa-park-main-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Haupteingang (main entrance) Europa-Park Rust.JPG" by Gabriel Rinaldi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Haupteingang_(main_entrance)_Europa-Park_Rust.JPG -->

entries.name: Phantasialand
entries.desc: A compact German park near Cologne known for densely detailed themed areas and heavily immersive coasters. Its theming is often cited among the best of any European park.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Phantasialand near Brühl, Germany
photo.credit: <!-- photo: phantasialand-aerial-view.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Phantasialand Brühl.JPG" by Wolkenkratzer · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Phantasialand_Br%C3%BChl.JPG -->

entries.name: Ocean Park Hong Kong
entries.desc: A marine-themed park on a hillside overlooking the South China Sea, combining an oceanarium and animal exhibits, including giant pandas, with thrill rides and a scenic cable car linking its two areas.

photo.alt: Ocean Park's cable car system linking its two areas, Hong Kong
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ocean-park-cable-car.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Ocean Park cable car system (Hong Kong).jpg" by Mk2010 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ocean_Park_cable_car_system_(Hong_Kong).jpg -->

entries.name: Gardaland
entries.desc: Italy's most visited theme park, on the shore of Lake Garda, mixing roller coasters and themed areas with a Sea Life aquarium. It is a major draw for the surrounding lake resort region.

photo.alt: The entrance fountain at Gardaland on Lake Garda, Italy
photo.credit: <!-- photo: gardaland-entrance-fountain.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Fontata Entrata Gardaland.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fontata_Entrata_Gardaland.jpg -->

entries.name: Tokyo Disneyland
entries.desc: The first Disney park built outside the United States, opened in 1983 near Tokyo. It closely echoes the classic American Disney layout and is one of the most visited theme parks in the world.

photo.alt: Cinderella Castle at Tokyo Disneyland, Urayasu, Chiba
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tokyo-disneyland-cinderella-castle.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Cinderella Castle, Tokyo Disneyland (9407195793).jpg" by Rob Young · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cinderella_Castle,_Tokyo_Disneyland_(9407195793).jpg -->

entries.name: Tokyo DisneySea
entries.desc: A nautically themed Disney park unique to Tokyo, organized into ports of call inspired by seafaring myth and history. It is widely praised for its elaborate theming and detailed environments.

photo.alt: The Mediterranean Harbor at Tokyo DisneySea, Urayasu, Chiba
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tokyo-disneysea-mediterranean-harbor.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Tokyo DisneySea Mediterranean Harbour View1 201306.jpg" by Wing1990hk · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tokyo_DisneySea_Mediterranean_Harbour_View1_201306.jpg -->

entries.name: Universal Studios Japan
entries.desc: A major movie-themed park in Osaka, home to Super Nintendo World and the Wizarding World of Harry Potter. It is among the most attended theme parks in Asia.

photo.alt: The iconic entrance building of Universal Studios Japan, Osaka
photo.credit: <!-- photo: universal-studios-japan-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Universal Studios Japan entrance.jpg" by Csiiiyu · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Universal_Studios_Japan_entrance.jpg -->

entries.name: Fuji-Q Highland
entries.desc: A park at the base of Mount Fuji known for extreme roller coasters that have held world records for speed and steepness, plus a large haunted-attraction complex. Rides offer sweeping views of the mountain.

photo.alt: Mount Fuji rising above Fuji-Q Highland's rides
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mount-fuji-fuji-q-highland.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Fuji-San and Fuji-Q.jpg" by sergejf · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fuji-San_and_Fuji-Q.jpg -->

entries.name: Xcaret Park
entries.desc: An eco-archaeological park on Mexico's Riviera Maya centered on underground rivers, wildlife and Maya cultural exhibits rather than mechanical rides. It is known for a large evening show of Mexican music and dance.

photo.alt: Cove and coastline at Xcaret Park on the Riviera Maya
photo.credit: <!-- photo: xcaret-park-cove-view.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Xcaret parque temático.jpg" by Karla Quiñonez · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Xcaret_parque_tem%C3%A1tico.jpg -->

entries.name: Efteling
entries.desc: One of the oldest theme parks in the world, in the Netherlands, built around fairy tales, folklore and fantasy in a wooded setting. It is the country's largest and most visited park.

photo.alt: Sprookjesbos (Fairytale Forest) at Efteling
photo.credit: <!-- photo: efteling-sprookjesbos-fairytale-forest.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Efeteling - Sprookjesbos - The Netherlands.jpg" by Hullie · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Efeteling_-_Sprookjesbos_-_The_Netherlands.jpg -->

meta: One of the World's Oldest Theme Parks

entries.name: Six Flags Qiddiya
entries.desc: A large theme park within Saudi Arabia's Qiddiya entertainment megaproject near Riyadh, planned around some of the world's tallest and fastest roller coasters. It is a flagship of the country's tourism ambitions.

photo.alt: Six Flags Qiddiya theme park near Riyadh, Saudi Arabia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: six-flags-qiddiya-theme-park.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Six Flags Qiddiya Theme Park, Saudi Arabia (2025).jpg" by Saudi Press Agency (SPA) · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Six_Flags_Qiddiya_Theme_Park,_Saudi_Arabia_(2025).jpg -->

entries.name: Universal Studios Singapore
entries.desc: A movie-themed park on the resort island of Sentosa, with zones spanning ancient Egypt, sci-fi and a Madagascar area. It is a central attraction of the Resorts World Sentosa complex.

photo.alt: Front entrance area of Universal Studios Singapore on Sentosa Island
photo.credit: <!-- photo: universal-studios-singapore-front.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Front Universal Singapore.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Front_Universal_Singapore.jpg -->

meta: Only Universal Park in Southeast Asia

entries.name: Everland
entries.desc: South Korea's largest theme park, set in the hills near Seoul, with themed zones, extensive seasonal gardens and a zoo. Its wooden coaster and safari areas are among its best-known features.

photo.alt: Entrance to Everland in Yongin, South Korea
photo.credit: <!-- photo: everland-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Entrance of Everland.jpg" by Christophe95 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entrance_of_Everland.jpg -->

entries.name: Lotte World
entries.desc: A large complex in central Seoul that pairs one of the world's biggest indoor theme parks with an outdoor island section on a lake. Its all-weather indoor design makes it a year-round attraction.

photo.alt: Daytime view of Lotte World in Seoul
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lotte-world-day-view.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Lotte World day view 1.jpg" by kallerna · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lotte_World_day_view_1.jpg -->

entries.name: PortAventura World
entries.desc: A large resort on Spain's Costa Daurada with themed lands and a separate Ferrari Land area. It is one of the most visited parks in Europe and a popular day trip from Barcelona.

photo.alt: Entrance to PortAventura World in Salou
photo.credit: <!-- photo: portaventura-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "PortAventura.jpg" by Uxuee123 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:PortAventura.jpg -->

entries.name: Liseberg
entries.desc: Scandinavia's largest amusement park, in central Gothenburg, mixing modern coasters with landscaped grounds and a popular Christmas market season. It is one of Sweden's most visited attractions.

photo.alt: Liseberg amusement park lit up at night, with Helix coaster and Ferris wheel, Gothenburg
photo.credit: <!-- photo: liseberg-park-night.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Helix night panorama" by Marcus Löfstrand · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Helix_night_panorama.jpg -->

entries.name: Ferrari World Abu Dhabi
entries.desc: A Ferrari-branded indoor theme park on Yas Island, housed under a vast red roof and home to one of the fastest roller coasters in the world. Attractions center on racing and the Ferrari marque.

photo.alt: Aerial view of the red-roofed Ferrari World building on Yas Island, Abu Dhabi
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ferrari-world-aerial.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Yas Marina Circuit + Ferrari World -Abu Dhabi" by Dapaan · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Yas_Marina_Circuit_+_Ferrari_World_-Abu_Dhabi.jpg -->

entries.name: Alton Towers
entries.desc: The United Kingdom's largest theme park, set in the grounds of a historic country estate and gardens in Staffordshire. It is known for landmark thrill coasters and its ruined manor house backdrop.

photo.alt: Ruined Gothic towers of the historic Alton Towers mansion, Staffordshire
photo.credit: <!-- photo: alton-towers-house-ruins.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "View of Alton Towers from roof" by Ed6767 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:View_of_Alton_Towers_from_roof.jpg -->

entries.name: Disneyland Park
entries.desc: The original Disney park, opened in 1955 and the only one designed under Walt Disney's direct supervision. Its themed lands and Sleeping Beauty Castle set the template for Disney parks worldwide.

photo.alt: Sleeping Beauty Castle at Disneyland Park, Anaheim
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sleeping-beauty-castle-disneyland.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Sleeping Beauty Castle DLR" by Carterhawk · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sleeping_Beauty_Castle_DLR.jpg -->

entries.name: Six Flags Magic Mountain
entries.desc: A thrill-focused park north of Los Angeles known for one of the largest collections of roller coasters of any park in the world. It draws visitors chasing high-speed and record-height rides.

photo.alt: Entrance arch of Six Flags Magic Mountain, Valencia, California
photo.credit: <!-- photo: six-flags-magic-mountain-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Six Flags Magic Mountain" by Jeremy Thompson · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Six_Flags_Magic_Mountain_(13208988393).jpg -->

entries.name: Universal Studios Hollywood
entries.desc: A theme park set on the grounds of a working film and television studio, famous for its Studio Tour tram ride through backlot sets. It combines movie-themed attractions with behind-the-scenes production areas.

photo.alt: Entrance plaza of Universal Studios Hollywood, Universal City, California
photo.credit: <!-- photo: universal-studios-hollywood-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Entrance of Universal Studios Hollywood, July 2022" by Benoît Prieur · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entrance_of_Universal_Studios_Hollywood,_July_2022.jpg -->

entries.name: Magic Kingdom
entries.desc: The centerpiece park of Walt Disney World, anchored by Cinderella Castle and organized into themed lands such as Fantasyland, Tomorrowland and Adventureland. It is consistently among the most-visited theme parks in the world.

photo.alt: Cinderella Castle at Magic Kingdom, Walt Disney World, Orlando
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cinderella-castle-magic-kingdom.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cinderella Castle" by Katie Rommel-Esham · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cinderella_Castle.jpg -->

entries.name: Universal Islands of Adventure
entries.desc: A Universal Orlando park built around highly themed islands, including the Wizarding World of Harry Potter's Hogsmeade and a Marvel Super Hero zone. It is known for its immersive environments and thrill rides.

photo.alt: Pharos Lighthouse and sign at the Port of Entry, Universal Islands of Adventure, Orlando
photo.credit: <!-- photo: islands-of-adventure-lighthouse.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "IoA Lighthouse 2024" by Blocks72 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:IoA_Lighthouse_2024.jpg -->

entries.name: Universal Epic Universe
entries.desc: The newest gateway park at Universal Orlando, divided into several immersive worlds spanning Nintendo, Harry Potter, monsters and fantasy themes. It marked a major expansion of the resort when it opened.

photo.alt: Aerial view of Celestial Park, the central hub of Universal Epic Universe
photo.credit: <!-- photo: epic-universe-celestial-park.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Celestial Park.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Celestial_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Cedar Point
entries.desc: A lakefront park on a Lake Erie peninsula, long regarded as a roller-coaster capital for its dense collection of record-setting rides. It is one of the oldest operating amusement parks in the United States.

photo.alt: Cedar Point main entrance sign reading "The Roller Coaster Capital of the World!" with coasters overhead
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cedar-point-main-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cedar Point main entrance (5437).jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cedar_Point_main_entrance_(5437).jpg -->

meta: Roller Coaster Capital of the World

entries.name: Dollywood
entries.desc: A park in the Great Smoky Mountains foothills co-owned by Dolly Parton, blending roller coasters with Appalachian crafts, live music and regional food. It is a major draw for the Pigeon Forge area.

photo.alt: Roller coaster train winding through wooded Smoky Mountains foothills at Dollywood
photo.credit: <!-- photo: dollywood-coaster-smoky-mountains.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Dollywood 2014 5.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Dollywood_2014_5.jpg -->
